edges.description: Loss of functional intrinsic factor prevents effective absorption of dietary cobalamin.
Defective cobalamin absorption lowers serum vitamin B12 and can increase methylmalonic acid and homocysteine.
Cobalamin depletion impairs DNA synthesis during erythropoiesis, producing megaloblastic anemia.
Cobalamin-dependent biochemical disruption impairs bone marrow erythropoiesis.
Systemic cobalamin deficiency from congenital absorption disorders can manifest with gastrointestinal symptoms.
Inherited cobalamin malabsorption can produce neurologic manifestations through downstream effects of cobalamin deficiency.
Megaloblastic erythropoietic failure produces the hallmark megaloblastic anemia phenotype.
Megaloblastic cobalamin-deficiency anemia falls within the macrocytic-anemia spectrum.
Severe cobalamin-related marrow failure can broaden beyond anemia to pancytopenia.
Low serum cobalamin reports failure of intrinsic-factor-dependent cobalamin absorption.
Increased methylmalonic acid reports impaired cobalamin-dependent metabolism in the biochemical deficiency state.
Elevated homocysteine reports impaired cobalamin-dependent one-carbon metabolism in the biochemical deficiency state.

nodes.description: Impaired intrinsic factor-dependent absorption lowers serum cobalamin and produces the downstream biochemical pattern of methylmalonic acid and homocysteine accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: Serum B12 was 61 (198-615 pmol/L).

evidence.reference_title: How can cobalamin injections be spaced in long-term therapy for inborn errors of vitamin B(12) absorption?
evidence.snippet: These disorders lead to intracellular Cbl depletion which in turn causes megaloblastic bone marrow failure, accumulation of homocysteine and methylmalonic acid (MMA), and methionine depletion.

nodes.description: Pathogenic CBLIF/GIF variation reduces or abolishes functional gastric intrinsic factor, preventing normal binding and intestinal uptake of dietary cobalamin.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of a 4-base deletion in the gene in inherited intrinsic factor deficiency
evidence.snippet: A 4-base deletion has been identified in the coding region of the gene for gastric intrinsic factor (IF) in an 11-year-old girl with severe anemia and cobalamin (Cbl) deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: Intrinsic factor deficiency (OMIM #261000, IFD) is a rare inherited disorder of vitamin B12 metabolism due to mutations in the gastric intrinsic factor (GIF) gene.

evidence.reference_title: Inherited cobalamin malabsorption. Mutations in three genes reveal functional and ethnic patterns.
evidence.snippet: We identified mutations in 126/154 unrelated cases (82%). Fifty-three of 126 cases (42%) were mutated in CUBN, 45/126 (36%) were mutated in AMN, and 28/126 (22%) had mutations in GIF.

nodes.description: Gastrointestinal symptoms can accompany inherited vitamin B12 absorption disorders, including hereditary intrinsic factor deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: How can cobalamin injections be spaced in long-term therapy for inborn errors of vitamin B(12) absorption?
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation reflects Cbl deficiency, with gastrointestinal symptoms, pancytopenia, and megaloblastic anemia.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: Homocysteine was high at 16.7 (5.0-12.0 umol/L).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated homocysteine reports impaired cobalamin-dependent one-carbon metabolism in the biochemical deficiency state.

nodes.description: Intrinsic-factor-dependent cobalamin transport failure depletes systemic vitamin B12 and disrupts downstream one-carbon and methylmalonic acid metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Inherited cobalamin malabsorption. Mutations in three genes reveal functional and ethnic patterns.
evidence.snippet: Recessive mutations in CUBN or AMN cause Imerslund-Gräsbeck Syndrome (IGS), while recessive mutations in GIF cause Intrinsic Factor Deficiency (IFD).

evidence.reference_title: Vitamin B12 Metabolism: A Network of Multi-Protein Mediated Processes
evidence.snippet: Thus, this review aims to compile current knowledge about the crucial proteins necessary to efficiently accumulate and process vitamin B12 in humans, presenting these systems as a multi-protein network.

nodes.description: Lifelong vitamin B12 replacement bypasses defective intrinsic factor availability and prevents or reverses hematologic and biochemical consequences of cobalamin malabsorption. Published cases include response to oral as well as parenteral vitamin B12, consistent with the treatment bypassing intrinsic factor-dependent absorption when sufficiently dosed.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: Oral or parenteral vitamin B12 has led to complete recovery of clinical parameters and vitamin B12 levels.

evidence.reference_title: How can cobalamin injections be spaced in long-term therapy for inborn errors of vitamin B(12) absorption?
evidence.snippet: Accurate diagnosis is always an emergency because early detection and treatment with life-long parenteral pharmacological doses of hydroxocobalamin are life saving and prevent further deterioration.

nodes.description: Macrocytic anemia can accompany the megaloblastic marrow phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary juvenile cobalamin deficiency caused by mutations in the intrinsic factor gene
evidence.snippet:
Hereditary juvenile megaloblastic anemia due to vitamin B
                    12
                    (cobalamin) deficiency is caused by intestinal malabsorption of cobalamin.

nodes.description: Megaloblastic anemia is the hallmark hematologic presentation of hereditary intrinsic factor deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: A high index of suspicion should be maintained if children present with megaloblastic anemia since GIF deficiency is a treatable disorder and newborn screening may not be able to detect this condition.

nodes.description: Vitamin B12 depletion disrupts erythroblast maturation, causing macrocytic or megaloblastic anemia and sometimes broader marrow failure.

nodes.description: Four-base deletion in the intrinsic factor gene reported in inherited intrinsic factor deficiency, predicted to cause premature termination.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of a 4-base deletion in the gene in inherited intrinsic factor deficiency
evidence.snippet: the nucleotide sequence analysis identified a 4-base deletion (c183_186delGAAT) spanning positions 104 to 107 in exon 2, resulting in premature termination of translation.

nodes.description: Compound heterozygous frameshift and nonsense variants reported in an East Asian patient with hereditary intrinsic factor deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary intrinsic factor deficiency in China caused by a novel mutation in the intrinsic factor gene—a case report
evidence.snippet: Gene test verified a c.776delA frame shift mutation in exon 6 combined with c.585C > A nonsense early termination mutation in exon 5 of GIF which result in the dysfunction of gastric intrinsic factor protein.

nodes.description: Compound heterozygous splice-site and deletion variants reported in Old Order Mennonite individuals with gastric intrinsic factor deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and Hematologic Manifestations of Gastric Intrinsic Factor (GIF) Deficiency: A Treatable Cause of B12 Deficiency in the Old Order Mennonite Population of Southwestern Ontario.
evidence.snippet: Mutation analysis of GIF revealed compound heterozygosity for a c.79+1G>A substitution and a c.973delG deletion in all three individuals.
